title: Sell Locally and Now Into Regional Markets: Temu’s Local Seller Program Explained (2026 Update)
description:

title: Why Gaming Advertising Belongs on More Ecommerce Media Plans
description:
Sponsor content is created on behalf of and in collaboration with Bazoom by DigitalCommerce360. Our editorial staff is not involved in the creation of the sponsored content. Gaming is not a single advertising channel. Brands treating it as one are leaving a measurable attention opportunity unaddressed. Ecommerce brands have built detailed playbooks for Meta, TikTok, […]

title: Retailers are rushing to build AI apps. It’s unclear if shoppers will use them
description: Retailers and brands are launching shopping apps within ChatGPT and Claude at a rapid pace. But whether or not shoppers will embrace them at a rapid pace remains to be seen.

title: Kohl’s delves into AI for gift finding, aiding associates
description: The retailer launched a conversational AI agent to help shoppers find gifts, and is evolving how associates engage with data using the technology.

title: Wayfair touts market share gains in retail’s toughest category
description: The home goods retailer also posted a healthy Q1 sales increase, but its struggles with profitability continued as net loss barely budged.

title: The Backroom: Redefining Pacsun
description: Brieane Olson talks about her book, “Co-created,” and her work as a merchant and now CEO in redefining the retailer for a new generation.

title: Amazon’s AWS boosts revenue growth in Q1 milestone
description:
Amazon Web Services (AWS) revenue growth reached 28% year over year during Amazon’s fiscal Q1. That was the segment’s fastest growth rate in 15 quarters — almost four years. It also represented $2 billion quarter-over-quarter growth. AWS has reached a $150 billion annualized revenue run rate, Amazon CEO Jassy said on the merchant’s Q1 earnings […]
The post Amazon’s AWS boosts revenue growth in Q1 milestone appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Ecommerce Trends: What Anthropic, OpenAI and Google are each doing in agentic commerce
description:
Throughout 2025 and the early months of 2026, OpenAI and Google have deployed steady trickles of updates to ChatGPT and Gemini’s ecommerce capabilities — even as their large language model (LLM) competitor Anthropic appeared to pursue other priorities with its generative artificial intelligence (AI) model Claude. Then, on April 24, Anthropic published the results of […]
The post Ecommerce Trends: What Anthropic, OpenAI and Google are each doing in agentic commerce appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Meta and Google ad revenues soar thanks to AI, but big picture is blurry
description: Google and Meta both posted impressive advertising growth in Q1, but their paths are diverging on the critical technology front.

title: How Nestlé turns creator content into brand-suitable ads at scale
description: The CPG giant is deploying a new tool from CreatorIQ and CreativeX that identifies and scores creator posts that can work as paid media.

title: Claire’s marks next chapter with Gen Alpha-focused ASMR campaign
description: “A Girl SMR at Claire's” includes ASMR set-ups, a Summer Sensory Shop, creator tie-ups and a presence on teen-focused social platform Coverstar.

title: Carter’s CMO on evolving marketing by pairing heritage with progress
description: The first creative under Global CMO Sarah Crockett captures a universal truth of youth sports: Parents around the world will eventually shout “Wrong goal!”

title: Your marketing automation isn’t broken, it’s overloaded
description:
As workflows pile up, automation systems become harder to trust, slowing campaigns and creating inconsistent results.
The post Your marketing automation isn’t broken, it’s overloaded appeared first on MarTech.

title: AI moved forward, marketing did not
description:
Marketers were early adopters of AI, but while it has evolved most teams are still stuck using it like a smarter autocomplete.
The post AI moved forward, marketing did not appeared first on MarTech.

title: Delegated authority is the missing layer in the AI martech stack
description:
Scaling AI without a clear governance layer risks uncoordinated liability. Delegated authority gives your agents the guardrails needed to act with confidence.
The post Delegated authority is the missing layer in the AI martech stack appeared first on MarTech.
